title: Poincianella caladenia
intro: Poincianella pyramidalis, also known as Poincianella caladenia, is a tree species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Poincianella caladenia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Poincianella caladenia is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being used in the preparation of traditional remedies such as decoctions and infusions.
